introSection: Tatsu is a Bolliger & Mabillard steel flying roller coaster located at Six Flags Magic Mountain in Valencia, California, USA. It is the tallest flying coaster in the world and is the joint-fastest alongside Flying Dinosaur at. Its cars allow riders to ride face-down in a prone position. It contains a pretzel loop, the tallest in the world.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Tatsu was announced on November 17, 2005. The ride opened on May 13, 2006.

Tatsu closed in September 2021 to be repainted with dark orange track and green supports.

In December 2021, repainting of Tatsu stopped and the ride was temporarily reopened, but repainting resumed in 2022. On March 12, 2022, Tatsu reopened with dark orange track and green supports.

In April 2022, a single-rider queue line was added.

sections.title: Name
sections.content: Tatsu is a Japanese word that means "Flying Beast".

fullText:
Tatsu is a Bolliger & Mabillard steel flying roller coaster located at Six Flags Magic Mountain in Valencia, California, USA. It is the tallest flying coaster in the world and is the joint-fastest alongside Flying Dinosaur at. Its cars allow riders to ride face-down in a prone position. It contains a pretzel loop, the tallest in the world.

Tatsu was announced on November 17, 2005. The ride opened on May 13, 2006.

Tatsu closed in September 2021 to be repainted with dark orange track and green supports.

In December 2021, repainting of Tatsu stopped and the ride was temporarily reopened, but repainting resumed in 2022. On March 12, 2022, Tatsu reopened with dark orange track and green supports.

In April 2022, a single-rider queue line was added.





Tatsu is a Japanese word that means "Flying Beast".
